channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Sat, 09 May 2026 16:42:29 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: UAE to decide on remote or in-person classes on May 10 as schools and universities remain on alert
item.description: UAE schools and universities await a crucial May 10 announcement to determine the return to in-person learning or continued remote education. This decision follows a nationwide safety assessment prompted by renewed regional tensions and emergency alerts. Authorities are prioritizing student and staff safety while ensuring educational continuity.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Arab Cinema shines at Cannes 2026 as Gulf Filmmakers lead major festival categories
item.description: Cannes 2026 highlights Arab and Middle Eastern cinema. Films from Palestine, Morocco, Egypt, and Iran feature prominently. This marks a shift towards culturally rooted storytelling. The festival emphasizes auteur cinema over Hollywood blockbusters. Arab filmmakers are gaining global recognition. This edition signifies a defining moment for regional cinema on the world stage.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: More hunting, fewer limits: Trump lifts restrictions in national parks and wilderness areas
item.description: The Trump administration is expanding hunting and fishing access on federal lands, including national parks and wildlife refuges, by removing existing restrictions. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum stated the policy is to open lands unless specific exceptions apply. This move aims to boost conservation and rural economies, despite concerns about visitor safety and science-based management.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'He was on the stairs': Iran claims Mojtaba Khamenei was knocked down by US blast but is 'recovering well'
item.description: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is reportedly in good health, with officials dismissing rumors of severe injuries following recent strikes. While acknowledging minor wounds, they insist he remains active in state affairs. Meanwhile, the US awaits Iran's response to a proposal aimed at ending the West Asia conflict, with officials expressing hope for a serious offer.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Starmer appoints Labour veterans Gordon Brown, Harriet Harman as advisers after local elections rout
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: ‘Voters frustrated by slow pace of change’: Sadiq Khan ramps up pressure on Starmer after London poll setback
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Inside a cave older than Egypt’s pyramids: 12,000-year-old stitched piece of elk hide may be the world’s oldest clothing
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: “Danger to society”: Internet erupts after Mason Hull reportedly walks free despite charges involving minors
item.description: Mason Hull, popularly known as “Hullo,” was reportedly released on bond after being charged with 15 counts related to possession of explicit material involving minors. JiDion shared alleged jail records online and criticized Hull’s release, calling him a “danger to society.” The YouTuber also warned Sarasota residents to “be careful.” Hull has not publicly addressed the allegations or the growing backlash surrounding the case.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: YouTuber Ethan Klein caught screenshotting Anisa Jomha’s Instagram Story during live poker event as clip explodes online
item.description: A viral Celebrity Poker Tour clip showed Ethan Klein seemingly screenshotting Anisa Jomha’s Instagram Story during a livestream. The moment quickly spread online because of Ethan Klein’s past public feud with Ian “iDubbbz” Carter and earlier comments involving Anisa Jomha.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: “Stop destroying innocent lives”: Laura Loomer erupts at Candace Owens over Erika Kirk controversy
item.description: Laura Loomer publicly attacked Candace Owens for allegedly spreading conspiracy theories about the death of Charlie Kirk and targeting his widow, Erika Kirk. Loomer called Owens a “modern-day cult leader” and accused her of “destroying innocent people’s lives.” Erika Kirk also pleaded with Owens to stop discussing her family. The feud has since escalated with accusations involving lawsuits, doxxing, and attacks on Owens’ husband, George Farmer.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Twitch CEO Dan Clancy announces stricter action against viewbotting
item.description: Twitch is implementing a new system to combat viewbotting, a practice where streamers artificially inflate viewer numbers. Starting in May, repeat offenders will face concurrent viewership caps based on their real traffic, with penalties escalating for persistent violations. The platform aims to create a fairer environment for legitimate creators by addressing fake engagement.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: ‘Four feet tall, wearing space suits and helmets’: FBI UFO files reveal bizarre claims
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 62-year-old Indian-American man to be denaturalized for $2.5 million fraud, false information

item.title: Eight-Pointed Star UFO Video: Pentagon releases 2013 mystery clip; Trump touts maximum transparency
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Texas woman running for Governor mocks Indians, says her new name is 'Pria'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Suspension of all kinetic activity': Trump announces 3-day Russia-Ukraine ceasefire, 1,000-prisoner swap
item.description: US President Trump announced a three-day Russia-Ukraine ceasefire from May 9-11, hoping for a long-term peace deal and a prisoner exchange. Despite this, fighting persisted, with both sides reporting attacks. Ukraine accused Russia of continuing assaults, while Russia claimed to intercept numerous drones. The truce follows Russia's Victory Day, a proposal Kyiv had previously dismissed.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by James Watson: “Worrying about complications before ruling out the possibility that the answer was simple would have been damned foolishness.”
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: ‘CIA knew Iran would shut down Hormuz...’: Ex-counterterror chief makes bombshell claim
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Queen Victoria: “Then, dear Mamma, I hope you will grant me the first request I make to you, as Queen. Let me be by myself for an hour.”
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Is the fight for Green Card worth it?' H-1B visa holder says he has decent money but can see parents once every two years
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
